title: Dockerfile Frontend, Build Context, Instructions, and Process Contracts · Delivery Case: Dockerfile Run Shell Exec
summary: A small accessible web application needs a readable Dockerfile whose build inputs, defaults, process model, and runtime files are explicit. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dockerfile run shell exec increment in reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish build-time RUN from runtime process configuration and choose shell or exec form with deliberate expansion and signal behavior.
Use FROM, WORKDIR, COPY, and justified ADD behavior with explicit ownership, paths, checksums, and context boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

steps.title: Predict Dockerfile Run Shell Exec · Theory Dockerfile Run Shell Exec
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory build sends a 780 MiB context containing .git and local.env, uses a floating base, copies the repository, runs as root, and wraps the process in /bin/sh. Redesign context and runtime contracts.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Dockerfile Run Shell Exec contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example build sends a 780 MiB context containing .git and local.env, uses a floating base, copies the repository, runs as root, and wraps the process in /bin/sh. Redesign context and runtime contracts. Competency probe: Distinguish build-time RUN from runtime process configuration and choose shell or exec form with deliberate expansion and signal behavior. A defensible solution applies Dockerfile Run Shell Exec, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: dockerfile-run-shell-exec — Distinguish build-time RUN from runtime process configuration and choose shell or exec form with deliberate expansion and signal behavior.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-dockerfile-build-context-instructions-5
  - competency: docker-worked-dockerfile-build-context-instructions-5
    artifact: Dockerfile
    decision: worked dockerfile build context instructions 5
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      FROM docker.io/library/alpine:3.22@sha256:verified AS runtime
      WORKDIR /app
      COPY --link public/ ./public/
      USER 10001:10001
      ENTRYPOINT ["/app/status"]
    verify: docker buildx build --check --progress=plain .
    failure: Reject a floating base, secret-bearing context, shell-form signal wrapper, root runtime, or copied repository wildcard.
    repair: Pin the reviewed base digest, shrink .dockerignore context, use explicit COPY, set numeric non-root identity, and test argv behavior.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Shell-form and exec-form commands have identical parsing, environment expansion, and PID behavior. This fails the Dockerfile Run Shell Exec contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish build-time RUN from runtime process configuration and choose shell or exec form with deliberate expansion and signal behavior.

content.code:
# Evidence: dockerfile-run-shell-exec-1kh465
  - competency: dockerfile-run-shell-exec
    artifact: Dockerfile
    decision: dockerfile run shell exec
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: invalidate one cache input without changing application source
    retained-proof: redacted configuration render and secret-rotation confirmation
    case-key: 1kh465
    change: |
      FROM docker.io/library/alpine:3.22@sha256:verified AS runtime
      WORKDIR /app
      COPY --link public/ ./public/
      USER 10001:10001
      ENTRYPOINT ["/app/status"]
    verify: docker buildx build --check --progress=plain .
    failure: Reject a floating base, secret-bearing context, shell-form signal wrapper, root runtime, or copied repository wildcard.
    repair: Pin the reviewed base digest, shrink .dockerignore context, use explicit COPY, set numeric non-root identity, and test argv behavior.
